servinfo.title: Air Bag System Construction
servinfo.ptxt: A 9-channel air bag system is employed for this model.
The 9-channel air bag system consists of the following components.

item.ptxt: Driver and passenger air bag (inflator) modules
Driver-side and passenger-side side-air bag (inflator) modules
Driver-side and passenger-side seat belt pretensioners
Driver-side and passenger-side curtain air bag (inflator) modules
Driver-side and passenger-side forward impact sensors
Driver-side and passenger-side front side-impact sensors
Driver-side and passenger-side rear side-impact sensors

servinfo.ptxt: To supplement protection offered by seat belts, the air bag system includes the following air bags: front and side air bags for driver and passenger seats, knee air bag for driver seat, and right and left side curtain air bags.
In addition to these air bags, the system also includes seat belt pretensioners for driver and passenger.

In the event of a frontal collision with an impact larger than the preset level, the front air bag (inflator) modules (stowed in the center of the steering wheel for the driver seat and under the top of instrument panel for passenger seat) and knee air bag (inflator) module are deployed. In the event of a sideward impact exceeding the preset level, the driver-side/passenger-side air bag inflator module as well as the curtain air bag inflator module (stowed in each side of roof) on the impact side are deployed.
The front seat belt pretensioners are activated whenever a frontal or sideward impact exceeding the preset level is detected to take up the sag in the seat belts.

The air bag system may be equipped with the passenger air bag deactivation switch. When this switch is set to “OFF” position, the front air bag for passenger seat is deactivated. This function is useful for mounting a child restraint on the passenger seat.

attention1.ptxt: Do not modify the front seats (driver and passenger) and do not install seat covers, cushions, etc. on them.
Otherwise, the air bag system may not operate properly and personal injury may result.

servinfo.ptxt: The air bag system is designed to activate only in severe frontal and sideward collisions. It is not designed to activate in rear impacts, rollovers, or minor frontal and sideward collisions; it would offer no protection in these types of accidents.
